title: Renato Cialente

infobox.value: (2 February 1897 – 25 November 1943) was an Italian stage and film actor. He appeared in 40 films between 1920 and 1943. His younger sister [Fausta Cialente](/wiki/Fausta_Cialente "Fausta Cialente") (1898–1994) was a novelist, journalist and political activist.[[1]](#cite_note-ruggiero-1)

Ruggiero, Nunzio (2017). ["CIALENTE, Fausta"](http://www.treccani.it/enciclopedia/fausta-cialente_%28Dizionario-Biografico%29/). *Dizionario Biografico degli Italiani* (in Italian). Rome: Treccani. Retrieved 15 August 2018.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian actor (1897–1943)

| Renato Cialente | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | (1897-02-02)2 February 1897  [Treviglio](/wiki/Treviglio "Treviglio"), [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy") |
| Died | 25 November 1943(1943-11-25) (aged 46)  [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), Kingdom of Italy |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 1920–1943 |

**Renato Cialente** (2 February 1897 – 25 November 1943) was an Italian stage and film actor. He appeared in 40 films between 1920 and 1943. His younger sister [Fausta Cialente](/wiki/Fausta_Cialente "Fausta Cialente") (1898–1994) was a novelist, journalist and political activist.[[1]](#cite_note-ruggiero-1)

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Renato_Cialente&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Born in [Treviglio](/wiki/Treviglio "Treviglio"), [Lombardy](/wiki/Lombardy "Lombardy"), Cialente began acting at his boarding school and made his professional debut with the stage company of [Ermete Zacconi](/wiki/Ermete_Zacconi "Ermete Zacconi").[[2]](#cite_note-bio-2) After having known the Russian actress [Tatyana Pavlova](/wiki/Tatyana_Pavlova "Tatyana Pavlova") he was among the first Italian actors to adopt the [Stanislavski's system](/wiki/Stanislavski%27s_system "Stanislavski's system"), and in the 1920s he started being considered a highly regarded actor by renowned dramatists such as [Luigi Pirandello](/wiki/Luigi_Pirandello "Luigi Pirandello") and [Pier Maria Rosso di San Secondo](/wiki/Pier_Maria_Rosso_di_San_Secondo "Pier Maria Rosso di San Secondo").[[2]](#cite_note-bio-2) In 1934 he formed a stage company with [Elsa Merlini](/wiki/Elsa_Merlini "Elsa Merlini") and gradually focused in the comedy genre.[[2]](#cite_note-bio-2)

Also active in cinema, Cialente tragically died in 1943 in [Nazi](/wiki/Nazi "Nazi") occupied Rome, when upon leaving the theatre where he had performed [Maxim Gorky](/wiki/Maxim_Gorky "Maxim Gorky")'s *[The Lower Depths](/wiki/The_Lower_Depths "The Lower Depths")* he was accidentally struck by a German military vehicle.[[2]](#cite_note-bio-2)

## Selected filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Renato_Cialente&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Selected filmography")]

* *[Beauty of the World](/wiki/Beauty_of_the_World "Beauty of the World")* (1927)
* *[The Girl with the Bruise](/wiki/The_Girl_with_the_Bruise "The Girl with the Bruise")* (1933)
* *[Paprika](/wiki/Paprika_(1933_Italian_film) "Paprika (1933 Italian film)")* (1933)
* *[The Lucky Diamond](/wiki/The_Lucky_Diamond "The Lucky Diamond")* (1934)
* *[The Little Schoolmistress](/wiki/The_Little_Schoolmistress_(1934_film) "The Little Schoolmistress (1934 film)")* (1934)
* *[I Love You Only](/wiki/I_Love_You_Only "I Love You Only")* (1935)
* *[Adam's Tree](/wiki/Adam%27s_Tree "Adam's Tree")* (1936)
* *[Pietro Micca](/wiki/Pietro_Micca_(film) "Pietro Micca (film)")* (1938)
* *[The Two Mothers](/wiki/The_Two_Mothers "The Two Mothers")* (1938)
* *[Battles in the Shadow](/wiki/Battles_in_the_Shadow "Battles in the Shadow")* (1938)
* *[A Thousand Lire a Month](/wiki/A_Thousand_Lire_a_Month "A Thousand Lire a Month")* (1939)
* *[The Dream of Butterfly](/wiki/The_Dream_of_Butterfly "The Dream of Butterfly")* (1939)
* *[The Knight of San Marco](/wiki/The_Knight_of_San_Marco "The Knight of San Marco")* (1939)
* *[Piccolo mondo antico](/wiki/Piccolo_mondo_antico_(film) "Piccolo mondo antico (film)")* (1941)
* *[The Last Dance](/wiki/The_Last_Dance_(1941_film) "The Last Dance (1941 film)")* (1941)
* *[A Pistol Shot](/wiki/A_Pistol_Shot_(1942_film) "A Pistol Shot (1942 film)")* (1942)
* *[The Countess of Castiglione](/wiki/The_Countess_of_Castiglione "The Countess of Castiglione")* (1942)
* *[The Queen of Navarre](/wiki/The_Queen_of_Navarre "The Queen of Navarre")* (1942)
* *[A Little Wife](/wiki/A_Little_Wife "A Little Wife")* (1943)
* *[Farewell Love!](/wiki/Farewell_Love! "Farewell Love!")* (1943)
* *[I'll Always Love You](/wiki/I%27ll_Always_Love_You_(1943_film) "I'll Always Love You (1943 film)")* (1943)
* *[Maria Malibran](/wiki/Maria_Malibran_(film) "Maria Malibran (film)")* (1943)
